notes: Multi Studio version 2.1.28: Added priority queueing for regenerated segments: clicking "Regenerate" on a block during active batch generation will now instantly prepend it to the top of the processing queue instead of appending it to the end., Added inline progress seekbar controls directly inside each block's top header card, allowing users to manually monitor, seek, and control individual block playback., Upgraded the segment card seekbar to utilize a premium, custom-styled Radix Slider component, creating a sleek, ultra-thin progress bar and a tiny, borderless, flat playhead (thumb) that spans the full available width for a beautiful, low-contrast UX. Expanded the vertical hit target (24px height) to make clicking/seeking effortless and added an interactive scale transition (+60%) when hovering the thumb playhead., Added real-time playback synchronization between the active segment card's progress bar and the bottom combined player, allowing cards to visually track progress during combined project runs., Added buttery-smooth 60fps playhead updates for both the local card player and the combined project player, replacing the choppy, low-frequency HTML5 Audio `timeupdate` event with a fluid `requestAnimationFrame` loop., Added a development configuration override file (`src-tauri/tauri.dev.json`) and a new package command (`bun run dev:desktop`) to automatically isolate the development database and cache directory from the production app, preventing resource sharing conflicts and data wipes., Fixed segment card metadata labels (character counts, ready durations, draft, error, and rendering states) to render in a unified, low-contrast `text-[10px]` font size, `font-mono` family, and `font-semibold` weight (matching the custom name styles), while maintaining full text selectability for copy/paste and using standard mixed case (e.g. "chars", "Ready") instead of full uppercase., Fixed a rare Tauri v2 event unlistener race condition crash (`TypeError: undefined is not an object evaluating 'listeners[eventId].handlerId'`) during component unmounting inside the file dropzone by wrapping cleanup calls in safe `try...catch` guards., Disabled all global configuration controls (Model, Voice, Director's Note style/pace/accent options, System Instructions text inputs, Instruction Presets, and Breathing Pause intervals) while audio generation is active to prevent conflicts and ensure rendering consistency., Updated the "Render all" behavior to perform a complete rebuild from the start (wiping and regenerating all segment audios fresh), while reserving the new "Resume render" button to continue rendering only draft and error blocks., Optimized timeline seek performance by moving the combined playback position state (`combinedTime`) into the Zustand store and rendering it inside a memoized sub-component, preventing parent re-renders and eliminating drag lag., Unified individual block playback and project-wide combined playback under a single synchronized audio engine (using the combined player), completely removing concurrent HTML5 Audio nodes, duplicate volume/playback states, and race condition freezes. Clicking play/pause or scrubbing the seekbar on any individual segment card now seamlessly updates and seeks the master combined player., Fixed audio overlap leaks when seeking or jumping playback across segments: starting a segment run now proactively pauses and cleans up any existing playing audio element before instantiating the next one., Cleaned up and removed all redundant player states, type definitions, and helper actions related to the old individual card playback system (such as `currentPlayingBlockId` and `setCurrentPlayingBlockId`) from the Zustand store and component trees, optimizing the app's memory footprint and state hydration lifecycle.
